verses.commentary: The marriage instruction aligns with Abraham's directive (24:3).
The same journey Abraham's servant made (chapter 24).
The Abrahamic blessing formally passed to Jacob.
Full covenant transfer.
The genealogy emphasizes family connections.
This stings given Esau's Hittite wives (26:34).
Contrast with Esau's disobedience in marriage.
Too late recognition of what pleased his father.
Partial obedience.
Alone, fleeing.
Certain place (hamakom, הַמָּקוֹם), the place, suggesting significance.
Stairway (sulam, סֻלָּם), ladder or staircase. Jesus refers to this (John 1:51).
The promise transferred directly.
The threefold promise (descendants, land, blessing) fully given to Jacob.
God commits to Jacob despite his deception.
God's presence was there all along.
Awesome (nora, נוֹרָא). House of God (bet Elohim, בֵּית אֱלֹהִים). Gate of heaven.
Set as pillar (matsevah, מַצֵּבָה), memorial. Consecration.
Named Bethel (Bet-El, בֵּית־אֵל), house of God.
Conditional vow. Basic needs.
Jacob bargains with God. His faith is immature but growing.
The vow shows both faith and self-interest.
